publications.title: NLP Based AI-Driven Resume Screening Solution for Efficient Candidate Selection
publications.description:
This project aims to revolutionize the hiring process by automating the screening of CVs, streamlining the selection of qualified candidates.

𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐨𝐦𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐇𝐢𝐫𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐜𝐞𝐬𝐬: Our project seeks to automate the hiring process by efficiently filtering necessary information from CVs, saving time and effort for recruiters.

𝐈𝐧𝐧𝐨𝐯𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞 𝐀𝐩𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐚𝐜𝐡: We tackle this challenge using cutting-edge Natural Language Processing (NLP) and Deep Learning techniques, ensuring accuracy and efficiency in candidate selection.

𝐀𝐝𝐝𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐂𝐮𝐫𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐧𝐠𝐞𝐬: Currently, recruiters are burdened with manually inputting selective information, leading to inconsistencies in job descriptions and skills required. Our solution mitigates this issue by dynamically extracting and analyzing crucial details from resumes.

𝐃𝐢𝐟𝐟𝐞𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝐄𝐱𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐨𝐥𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧𝐬: Unlike traditional methods that rely on manual screening, our solution leverages advanced NLP algorithms to intelligently categorize and rank resumes based on their relevance to the job description.

𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐅𝐞𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐬: Our system utilizes NLP to extract relevant information from unstructured resume formats, employing a content-based recommendation system. By utilizing a Vector space model and similarity check between job descriptions and resumes, we ensure accurate candidate ranking.
publications.publisher: International Congress on Information and Communication Technology

publications.title: Neural Image Caption Generation with Visual Attention : Enabling Image Accessibility for the Visually Impaired
publications.description:
🔍💡 Our Vision: Bridging the Accessibility Gap for Visually Impaired Individuals

In today's digital age, images play a crucial role in conveying messages and emotions online. However, this presents a significant challenge for the millions of visually impaired individuals worldwide who cannot perceive visual content. Our publication proposes an innovative solution to address this issue: a neural image caption generation model with visual attention.

🌟 Key Features:

The model uses a combination of CNNs and RNNs to convert image content into aural descriptions.
We leverage a text-to-speech API to transform textual descriptions into audio, enabling visually impaired individuals to comprehend visual information through sound.
🎯 Our Objective:

Our primary goal is to generate accurate and effective captions that vividly describe the visual elements of an image.
We aim to achieve high precision and descriptiveness through exhaustive experimentation and evaluation.
We will compare our model's generated captions with ground truth captions annotated by humans to ensure accuracy.
✅ Advancing Digital Inclusion:

Our research holds the potential to significantly improve the online experience for visually impaired individuals.
By allowing them to interact with visual content, we promote digital inclusion and equality.
Enriching the lives of millions, our work fosters meaningful image-based interactions for visually impaired individuals.
publications.publisher: International Journal of Scientific Research in Science, Engineering and Technology (IJSRSET), Volume 10, Issue 3

publications.title: Enhanced Credit Card Fraud Detection: A Novel Approach Integrating Bayesian Optimized Random Forest Classifier with Advanced Feature Analysis and Real-time Data Adaptation
publications.description:
🔍 Cutting-Edge Fraud Detection: Enhancing Credit Card Security with Machine Learning 🛡️💳

💰 Credit card fraud poses significant challenges in the financial industry, causing substantial financial losses for both individuals and businesses. However, machine learning algorithms equipped with pattern recognition capabilities have emerged as powerful tools in combating this issue.

📑 Our recent paper introduces a groundbreaking approach, leveraging an enhanced Bayesian random forest classifier, to significantly enhance credit card fraud detection. By addressing the limitations of conventional random forest classifiers through Bayesian optimization of hyperparameters, we've achieved remarkable results.

🔬 Using a publicly available credit card fraud dataset, we conducted extensive experiments to evaluate the effectiveness of our proposed strategy. The outcomes speak volumes: an exceptional area under the curve (AUC) of 0.99 and an impressive accuracy of 99.6%. These results surpass traditional random forest and benchmark techniques, solidifying the superiority of our classifier.

💡 Moreover, our research fosters transparency and interpretability by offering valuable insights into the underlying components that contribute to fraud detection. We acknowledge the limitations and identify avenues for improvement in the future.

🌐 Furthermore, our methodology extends beyond credit card fraud detection, as its application in other fraud detection scenarios can be explored. We also emphasize the importance of scalability and robustness testing on larger and more diverse datasets.

🔒 Ultimately, our study contributes to the development of reliable and efficient fraud detection tools for the financial industry. Fraud analysts and investigators can leverage our proposed Bayesian-optimized random forest classifier as a decision support tool, offering versatility in addressing various fraud detection challenges that extend beyond credit card theft.
publications.publisher: International Journal for Innovative Engineering and Management Research

experience.title: Junior Processing Officer
experience.summary:
Settlements: Reconciliation of the client’s file for the cardholder activity and ATM terminal activity with the file from associations’ (Visa/Mastercard) file for the previous day’s transactions to check for exceptions. Exceptions, if any, will be reported to the client and funding entries to the associations will be posted in Systematics. If no exceptions, funding entries to the associations will be posted in Systematics.
Adjustments: Any adjustments received from the associations will be processed and debits or credits posted to the association as per the instructions given in the adjustments.
Disputes (ATM): Receiving the disputes forms from the customer through the clients. Checking in Cardpac or Systematics, whether the transaction for which the customer is disputing is actually appearing in the customer’s account and whether we have processed any credits for the disputed transaction already to the customer (if we had received the credit from the association for the disputed transaction, before getting the dispute form from the customer, we would have processed the credit to the customer.), if yes, the case is closed, else, We’ll be giving a temporary credit to the customer’s account in Systematics/Cardpac for the disputed transaction and raise a claim with the association for the same. The denial or honouring of the claim will be received from the associations through adjustments.
Proofs & MIS: Every month, the client will anounce a date for surprise proofing of all accounts in which we are posting entries. We need to prove those accounts’ closing balance as of that date, providing the break ups for that and reasons for items aging in the accounts. Also have to submit various types of MIS everymonth as required by the clients.
Environment: Mainframes – Cardpac and Systematic

projects.title: Deposit Finder Release 5 UAT - Corporate Banking Division
projects.description:
The Royal Bank of Scotland (RBSG) is one of the largest banks in the world, both in terms of revenue and human capital. With the introduction of the Financial Conduct Authority in 2013, a new regulatory framework is being introduced.  The new group policy for Conduct Risk consists of 26 chapters including “Product Sales and Advice”.  This chapter requires a number of changes to be made to the Deposit (including current accounts) sales process and also the sales and lead generation process for non-B&C products. 

To ensure RBSG comply with the way deposit products are sold, the Deposit Finder therefore had 2 objectives:-

•	Provide a way to ensure a structured conversation with a customer occurs, that identifies the customer's needs and enables RBSG to identify suitable deposit products for the customer.  With face to face interactions it's important that the customer experience is enhanced by the customer being able to see the features and benefits of the products that the decision tree has highlighted as being suitable.

•	Evidence of the conversation must always be provided, with the response provided by the customers, and the output needs to be stored in an easily accessible location.

Deposit Finder is now in full use in production, on both the desktop and iPad, v ia Good for Enterprise (GfE) platform, for both Natwest and RBS brands. There are several subsequent releases planned which will address outstanding required functionality and defect fixes.

This release is the User Authentication or UA Release (Release Five) User Authenication for Deposit Finder and CBD Lead Form is done using non-ECD App Security (an existing e-Flex application used for user authentication). 

UA describes how an administrator uses App Security to add or remove users and other administrators on these applications. In addition, a production fix was included in this release. This related to a date field in the report section on summary screen.

projects.title: Anti Money Laundering - Portfolio Monitoring Services - Corporate Banking Division
projects.description:
The Royal Bank of Scotland (RBSG) is one of the largest banks in the world, both in terms of revenue and human capital. At present Corporate Banking Division (CBD) has no automated process in place to conduct on going Customer Due Diligence (CDD) reviews for the lifetime of the customer relationship following key amendments to customers and entities. These issues need to be addressed in order to meet Group AML Policy/ Compliance guidelines and ensure that the required procedures are in place for effective Periodic Reviews (PR) and Event Driven Reviews (EDR) of customers within CBD scope. The CBD AML Monitoring Service (PMS) aims to monitor the RBS CBD customer portfolio to assist in keeping KYC data up-to-date and performing AML due-diligence checking such as verification of key individuals. The purpose of System  testing was to ensure the validity of the data within the alerts provided in PMS flat file by Experian as part of the PMS solution.

The CBD AML Monitoring Service aims to monitor the Bank’s customer portfolio to assist in keeping KYC data up-to-date and performing AML due-diligence checking such as verification of key individuals. This service separates into several components:

•	Monitoring of the customer portfolio for changes in Companies House data, such as Key Principals (KPs), Ultimate Beneficial Owners (UBOs) and registered addresses
•	On-going assessment of the differences between the RBS Bank’s internal data and that filed at Companies House
•	Prioritisation of these data differencesalerts generated via PMS (resulting from changes to Companies House data) for review

The main objective of SIT was to test all the software components together in an integrated environment, and validate that the solution provided will support the end-to-end business processes as per the functional specification documents.

experience.summary:
Infrastructural Financial Markets 
Understanding twenty-first-century global financial integration requires a two-part background.  The course on the Global Financial Markets, Institutions, and Infrastructure begin its description of how we created a financially-integrated world by first examining the history of financial globalization, from Roman practices and Ottoman finance to Chinese standards, the beginnings of corporate practices, and the advent of efforts to safeguard financial stability.  It then describes the architecture itself by analyzing its parts, such as markets, institutions, and infrastructure.  The contributions of sovereign funds, auditing regulation, loan markets, property rights, compensation practices, Islamic finance, and others to the global architecture are closely examined.  For those seeking substantial, authoritative descriptions and summaries, this volume will replace books, journals, and other information sources with single, easy-to-use reference work.
Managerial Finance 
This introductory course in finance sets the groundwork for financial decision-making in two primary contexts. First, it covers how firms choose to invest in new projects. Second, it outlines principles by which portfolio managers select financial securities, such as stocks.
Corporate Finance — Summer 2022
This introductory course in corporate finance has three main components. First, it develops the tools that are used to value investment projects and companies (valuation). Second, it addresses the basic issues involved in how firms should raise funds for their investments (financing). Third, it evaluates how investment and financing decisions are related.

Course content
Blockchain 1.0 represents the early introduction of the technology, focused primarily on financial transactions.
Blockchain 2.0 expands capabilities to support "smart contracts" that replace traditional paper contracts. Small applets within the chain create, validate, monitor and enforce the terms and conditions of an agreement. Such types of blockchain are increasingly used in supply chain management operations where goods are bought, sold and transported.
Blockchain 3.0 boosts interoperability and scalability to support decentralized applications, dubbed dApps. A dApp runs on the back end across a decentralized P2P network; blockchain is active on the front end to make calls to the dApp running on the back end.
